diagrams.title: Singly Linked List Node Structure
diagrams.description: Shows the basic structure of a node in a singly linked list with data and next pointer
elements: Next Pointer (points to next node or NULL)
diagrams.purpose: Understanding fundamental building block of linked lists

diagrams.title: Insertion at Beginning
diagrams.description: Step-by-step visualization of inserting a new node at the beginning of a linked list
elements: Step 1: Create new node with data X
Step 2: Set new node's next to current head
Step 3: Update head to point to new node
Result: New node becomes first element
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing the O(1) insertion operation at head

diagrams.title: Linked List vs Array Memory Layout
diagrams.description: Comparison of memory allocation between linked lists and arrays showing contiguity differences
elements: Linked List: scattered nodes with arrows connecting them
Array indices shown, linked list shows pointers
diagrams.purpose: Understanding why linked lists are more memory efficient but slower to access

diagrams.title: Doubly Linked List
diagrams.description: A doubly linked list showing both previous and next pointers in each node
elements: Arrows showing forward and backward connections
Head points to first, NULL at both ends
